Market Yesterday (Thursday) - U.S. markets moved lower on Thursday as continued weakness in semiconductor and AI-related stocks weighed on investor sentiment. Although many companies reported solid earnings, selling pressure in technology more than offset the positive news, pushing the Nasdaq down sharply while the S&P 500 and Dow posted more modest declines. Following yesterday's close, TurboRegime's RiskOn signal remained intact, but the margin above a potential RiskOff transition narrowed considerably.

Market Today (Friday) - U.S. stock futures are pointing to another weaker opening as the semiconductor selloff continues, Netflix trades lower following weaker-than-expected guidance, and investors reassess the strength of the AI-driven rally. For TurboRegime subscribers, today's session carries added significance. The TReg Regime Signal has narrowed to just 0.014, placing the model near its decision threshold. While RiskOn remains in effect, another meaningful decline today could be enough to confirm a transition to RiskOff prior to the closing bell. As always, TurboRegime will wait for near end-of-day confirmation before making any allocation changes, avoiding the false signals that can occur during intraday trading. 

Current Risk Regime - RiskOn remains in effect, active since July 1. Following yesterday's close, the current trade stands at a gain of 0.5%, while the maximum drawdown since entering the position remains just -0.2%. Now entering its eleventh trading day, the trade continues to exhibit relatively low volatility. However, the TReg Regime Signal has weakened over the past several sessions and now sits at 0.014, indicating that market conditions have become less supportive than they were earlier in the trade. While no regime change has been confirmed, today's closing prices will be particularly important in determining whether the current allocation remains appropriate heading into next week.

Hold or Trade - HOLD. No portfolio changes are indicated following yesterday's close. Current allocation remains 85% SPY, 15% SHV, and 0% SPXL. If a RiskOff signal is confirmed after today's close, TurboRegime would recommend shifting to a more defensive allocation of 60% SPY and 40% SHV. It is important to understand that RiskOff is not an exit from the market. Instead, it is a disciplined risk-management adjustment that reduces portfolio volatility while maintaining meaningful participation in the equity market should conditions stabilize or improve.  Website Dashboard is updated every hour (0:45 past), so stay tuned.

Live Performance - Live tracking has now reached 107 days since beginning on April 1, 2026. Through yesterday's close, TurboRegime continues to significantly outperform a buy-and-hold investment in SPY while navigating multiple market environments with only six completed trades. Equally encouraging, live execution continues to closely mirror historical expectations. Since April 1, the live portfolio has returned 31.8%, compared with 32.4% for the historical backtest over the same period—a variance of just 0.6 percentage points. While small differences between live trading and backtesting are expected because of execution timing and market conditions, the close alignment observed to date provides continued confidence that the strategy is performing as designed under real-world market conditions.

General Commentary - One of the strengths of TurboRegime is that it does not require investors to predict what the market will do next. Today's session may ultimately result in no change at all, or it could produce a confirmed transition to RiskOff. Either outcome is acceptable because the objective is not to forecast the future—it's to respond consistently to changing market conditions. When conditions become less favorable, the strategy becomes more defensive. When conditions improve, it increases exposure. By allowing objective market data—not emotion—to drive allocation decisions, TurboRegime seeks to remove one of the largest obstacles to long-term investment success: human behavior.
